## Summary

- The discussion covered AI adoption, process improvement and how to choose useful starting points.
- The discussion also covered strategy, and, document, draft.
- Right, let's run through the AI programme items quickly.

## Discussion Points

### AI adoption, change management and process improvement

#### Discussion points

- The discussion covered AI adoption, process improvement and how to choose useful starting points. _(Sources: responsibility#1, question#1, question#2, question#3)_

#### Responsibilities

- AI opportunities need to solve a real process problem and keep responsible people in the decision loop. _(Sources: responsibility#1)_

#### Evidence required

- Process maps, opportunity registers, roadmaps and improvement criteria are supporting evidence. _(Sources: responsibility#1)_

#### Risks

- AI work may fail to land if it is built on top of poor workflows or does not solve a real problem. _(Sources: responsibility#1)_

#### Open questions

- Open questions remain around opportunity fit, adoption barriers or how the process should change. _(Sources: question#1, question#2, question#3)_

### Strategy, And, Document

#### Discussion points

- The discussion also covered strategy, and, document, draft. _(Sources: evidence_request#3, action#1, action#2, evidence_request#1, question#1)_

### Additional transcript-supported discussion points

- Right, let's run through the AI programme items quickly. _(Sources: raw#1)_
- The repeatable AI use case library can be marked complete now because use cases one and two are live and documented. _(Sources: raw#2)_
- There is polish work left, but the deliverable exists. _(Sources: raw#3)_
- The form exists but routing is not working properly yet, so it is not operational. _(Sources: raw#4)_
- Two reviews have gone through it, but the templates are not final. _(Sources: raw#5)_
- Keone starts next week, but realistically it is later in June before we touch it. _(Sources: raw#6)_
- The strategy document itself does not exist yet. _(Sources: raw#7)_
- Emma is chasing and said she would follow up this week. _(Sources: raw#8)_
- Version one was completed yesterday. _(Sources: raw#9)_
- Status is pending leadership review. _(Sources: raw#10)_

## Decisions

- No decisions detected.

| Follow up innovation grant feedback. | Ciara Griffin | Before next week | raw_action#4 |
| Validate intake workflow routing. | Ciara Griffin | Before next week | raw_action#5 |

- Open questions remain around opportunity fit, adoption barriers or how the process should change. _(Sources: question#1, question#2, question#3)_

## Summary

- The discussion covered the intended structure, running order and flow of the session.
- The discussion covered AI adoption, process improvement and how to choose useful starting points.
- The discussion also covered the, and, but, can.

## Discussion Points

### Meeting agenda, structure and flow

#### Discussion points

- The discussion covered the intended structure, running order and flow of the session. _(Sources: responsibility#5, question#1)_

#### Responsibilities

- The team needs to keep the session structure clear and easy to follow. _(Sources: responsibility#5)_

#### Evidence required

- Agenda notes, running-order comments and presenter handover points are supporting evidence. _(Sources: responsibility#5)_

#### Risks

- If the structure is unclear, the session may feel rushed or difficult to follow. _(Sources: responsibility#5)_

#### Open questions

- Open questions remain around flow, handovers or what should be covered in each part. _(Sources: question#1)_

### AI adoption, change management and process improvement

#### Discussion points

- The discussion covered AI adoption, process improvement and how to choose useful starting points. _(Sources: responsibility#1, responsibility#5, question#1, responsibility#2)_

#### Responsibilities

- AI opportunities need to solve a real process problem and keep responsible people in the decision loop. _(Sources: responsibility#1, responsibility#5, responsibility#2)_

#### Evidence required

- Process maps, opportunity registers, roadmaps and improvement criteria are supporting evidence. _(Sources: responsibility#1, responsibility#5, responsibility#2)_

#### Risks

- AI work may fail to land if it is built on top of poor workflows or does not solve a real problem. _(Sources: responsibility#1, responsibility#5, responsibility#2)_

#### Open questions

- Open questions remain around opportunity fit, adoption barriers or how the process should change. _(Sources: question#1)_

### The, And, But

#### Discussion points

- The discussion also covered the, and, but, can. _(Sources: action#1, responsibility#3, responsibility#5, responsibility#1, responsibility#2)_

### Additional transcript-supported discussion points

#### Discussion points

- Let's walk through the webinar flow. _(Sources: raw#1)_
- We open with the welcome, then Jack explains the AI discovery workshop, then Conor shows the complaints triage demo. _(Sources: raw#2)_
- The key message is that AI work should start with a defined business problem. _(Sources: raw#3)_
- Otherwise people build tools that nobody uses. _(Sources: raw#4)_
- Use the phrase responsible adoption of AI in a GXP setting. _(Sources: raw#5)_
- That is clearer than saying regulatory input. _(Sources: raw#6)_
- The slides are still too text-heavy. _(Sources: raw#7)_
- Otherwise I jump too quickly into the screen. _(Sources: raw#8)_
- This is the first time we mention the offering, but it should not feel salesy. _(Sources: raw#9)_

### Decision context

#### Discussion points

- Keep the tone educational. _(Sources: raw_decision#2)_

## Decisions

- Use the phrase responsible adoption of AI in a GXP setting. _(Sources: raw_decision#1)_
- Keep the tone educational. _(Sources: raw_decision#2)_

| simplify the methodology slide. | Jack Cunningham | Before live webinar | raw_action#4 |
| refine the demo introduction. | Conor Flynn | Before live webinar | raw_action#5 |
| practise the delivery before the live session. | Jack Cunningham | Before live webinar | raw_action#6 |

## Follow-up / Open Questions

- Open questions remain around flow, handovers or what should be covered in each part. _(Sources: question#1)_
- Open questions remain around opportunity fit, adoption barriers or how the process should change. _(Sources: question#1)_
